name: Baking Tips Guide for Yeast Breads
recipeInstructions:
1. Loaf rises then falls (cratered loaf)
- Decrease water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Increase salt by 1/4 tsp.
- Decrease yeast by 1/2 tsp.
- Flour is too fine or ingredients are not measured properly.
2. Loaf sides caved in
- Decrease water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Increase salt by 1/4 tsp.
- Decrease yeast by 1/2 tsp.
- Ingredients are not measured properly.
3. Loaf rises too high (mushroom loaf)
- Decrease water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Increase salt by 1/4 tsp.
- Decrease sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
- Decrease flour by 2 tbsp.
- Decrease yeast by 1/2 tsp.
- Used wrong type of yeast instead of dry granular
- Ingredients are not measured properly.
4. Loaf does not rise enough
- Increase water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Decrease salt by 1/4 tsp.
- Increase sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
- Increase yeast by 1/2 tsp.
- Not enough water or milk
- Flour is too old or low in gluten content or fine
- Used wrong type of yeast instead of dry granular
- Accidentally measured salt in tbsp. instead of tsp.
5. Flat loaf, little to no rising, short and heavy
- Liquid is too hot or cold
- Flour is too old or low in gluten content
- No yeast added or yeast is too old
- Used wrong type of yeast instead of dry granular
- Accidentally measured salt in tbsp. instead of tsp.
- Ingredients are not measured properly
- Breadmaker unplugged or power interruption
6. Crust too dark
- Decrease sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
7. Crust too light
- Decrease salt by 1/4 tsp.
- Increase sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
- Ingredients are not measured properly
- Breadmaker unplugged or power interruption
- Incorrect bread setting was used
8. Uncooked or partially cooked
- Decrease water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Breadmaker unplugged or power interruption
- Incorrect bread setting was used.
9. Overcooked
- Decrease sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
- Incorrect bread setting was used.
10. Not mixed or partially mixed
- Not enough water or milk
- Flour is too fine
- Ingredients are not measured properly
- Breadmaker unplugged or power interruption
- Baking pan is not properly seated inside the breadmaker
- Kneading blade is not installed correctly.
11. Loaf is soggy
- Bread is not removed from baking pan soon after baking.
12. Gnarly knotted top
- Increase water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Decrease flour by 2 tbsp.
- Not enough water or milk
- Ingredients are not measured properly.
13. Loaf core texture open, coarse, or uneven
- Increase salt by 1/4 tsp.
- Decrease yeast by 1/2 tsp.
- Forgotten salt
- Ingredients are not measured properly.
14. Loaf core texture heavy and dense
- Increase sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
- Decrease flour by 2 tbsp.
- Increase yeast by 1/2 tsp.
- Used wrong type of yeast instead of dry granular
- Ingredients are not measured properly.
15. Burning odor during operation
- Ingredient spilled inside breadmaker and heating element.
- See mushroom loaf solutions for bread mushroomed over edge of bread pan, crust burned.
16. High altitude adjustment
- Decrease water or milk by 2 tbsp.
- Decrease sugar or honey by 1 tsp.
- Decrease yeast by 1/2 tsp.
17. Breadmaker cannot be programmed or started
- Breadmaker unplugged or power interruption
- Breadmaker too hot, allow to cool 15 minutes between baking cycles
- Breadmaker malfunctioning.
18. Loaf burned completely
- Breadmaker malfunctioning.
